arguments.task: Open the LinkedIn Marketing developer documentation and gather official evidence about: 1) what LinkedIn Ads / Marketing APIs are, 2) authentication method(s) and auth flow, 3) whether API access is open self-serve or requires approval/partnership/application review, 4) API surface type (REST and/or GraphQL), 5) rate limit documentation, and 6) any official or first-party references relevant to MCP/server availability. Prefer Microsoft Learn / LinkedIn official docs, auth docs, rate limit docs, access program pages, and official GitHub pages if linked. Return the exact page URLs and concise extracted findings.

input.thought: Start a focused browser run on official LinkedIn Marketing docs to extract authoritative evidence for API buildability.

raw_result.description: LinkedIn Ads provides marketing APIs for managing advertising, audiences, analytics, and related campaign workflows on LinkedIn.
raw_result.auth_notes: LinkedIn documentation states LinkedIn APIs use OAuth 2.0, with Marketing APIs specifically requiring the 3-legged member authorization code flow rather than 2-legged application auth.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official LinkedIn Marketing docs indicate developers must apply for API access and that some Marketing APIs are restricted or require additional qualification criteria; access is approval-based rather than open self-serve.

api_surface.breadth: Official Marketing documentation covers advertising and related marketing endpoints, and shared API concepts describe LinkedIn APIs as Rest.li/REST-style request methods rather than GraphQL.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party MCP server evidence was verified in the gathered documentation. This is treated as unverified absence and should be human-reviewed if MCP availability is important.

raw_result.blocker: API access is approval-gated, so a toolkit can likely be built only after manual application/approval and potentially product-specific qualification for the needed Marketing APIs.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: A rate-limiting section/page was identified in the documentation navigation during browsing, but no specific numeric limits were captured from the evidence gathered. Human review is needed before asserting exact quotas.
raw_result.notable_signals: Microsoft Learn hosts the official LinkedIn Marketing docs; the quick start emphasizes applying for access before using APIs, the access docs distinguish approved versus restricted access, and the auth docs note Marketing APIs require 3-legged OAuth.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Numeric rate-limit details were not captured, and MCP availability was not directly verified from a first-party page; these fields remain partially uncertain.
